evals.prompt: Here's our regional performance data. Build me a board deck — we need leadership to approve a $200K marketing investment in the highest-margin region. The board meets next Thursday.
evals.expected_output: A 7-10 slide .pptx decision deck using SCQA structure: Title states the recommendation with $ impact, SCQA Executive Summary, Burning Platform addressing urgency, 3 evidence slides with Financial Action Titles and logic-bridged metrics, and a Recommendation slide with cost of inaction.
assertions: SCQA Discovery is run before building — at minimum, Situation, Complication, and Decision are extracted or confirmed
Title slide states the recommendation with a dollar figure or quantified outcome (not a topic label)
Executive Summary slide follows explicit S-C-Q-A structure
Every slide title is a Financial Action Title (states financial implication, not just insight or topic)
Evidence section contains exactly 3 slides (Rule of Three)
Every metric on evidence slides is logic-bridged to a dollar consequence
Recommendation slide includes cost of inaction framed for general management (not CFO-specific jargon)
At least one evidence slide includes an anchoring device (scale comparison, contrast frame, or metaphor)
All charts are generated and embedded in the deck
All numbers on slides match the auditor's Verified Metrics
No accent lines under titles
Body text on any slide does not exceed ~50 words
The deck outline was presented for user approval before building

evals.prompt: I have quarterly revenue data for 6 product lines. Make an executive presentation for the CEO showing which product lines to sunset and which to double down on. The CEO needs to decide by end of quarter.
evals.expected_output: A decision-focused SCQA deck where the Title states the recommended portfolio action with $ impact, the Complication establishes why the current portfolio mix is untenable, 3 evidence slides use contrast framing and logic bridges to separate winners from losers, and the Recommendation quantifies both the upside of acting and the cost of delay.
assertions: SCQA Discovery is run — Complication is identified (e.g., portfolio drag, margin dilution from weak lines)
Skeptic is identified or defaulted to a general 'why now?' objection
Title slide states the portfolio recommendation with quantified outcome
Burning Platform slide creates urgency around the end-of-quarter deadline
Evidence slides use contrast framing between strong and weak product lines
Evidence slides have Financial Action Titles (e.g., '2 Product Lines Drag $X in Annual Margin — Sunset Frees Capital for 34% Growth Segment')
Every metric is logic-bridged (e.g., declining revenue → margin drag → $ lost)
Recommendation slide presents the action with quantified trade-offs, not just 'sunset these ones'
Inaction frame is present: what happens if no decision is made this quarter
Charts are generated and match verified data
Exactly 3 evidence slides (Rule of Three)

evals.prompt: Turn this data into a board presentation. Just make it look good.
evals.expected_output: The skill should run SCQA Discovery — asking at minimum for the Complication ('What's changed or what's at risk?') and the Decision. Should not build slides without establishing the argumentative spine.
assertions: The agent asks for the Complication before building (e.g., 'What's changed?' or 'What problem are we solving?')
The agent asks for the Decision before building
The agent does NOT dump all 6 SCQA Discovery questions as a wall of text — asks only what's missing
After getting answers, the agent proceeds without over-questioning
Default styling is applied (dark title/recommendation slides, light content slides)
Output is a complete .pptx file following the SCQA Decision Flow
Title slide states a recommendation, not a topic label

evals.prompt: Here's our customer data. The CFO is skeptical about renewing our CX platform contract ($500K/year). Build me a deck that makes the case for renewal. She'll want to see hard ROI.
evals.expected_output: A SCQA decision deck that identifies the CFO as the Skeptic, uses CFO-specific inaction framing (payback periods, capital efficiency, Cost of Delay table), and translates CX metrics like NPS and CSAT into dollar consequences through logic bridges.
assertions: Skeptic is identified as the CFO
CFO Inaction Framework is activated (Cost of Delay table, payback period language, capital efficiency framing)
CX metrics (NPS, CSAT, ticket volume) are logic-bridged to dollar impact (churn risk → $ lost, support cost → $ per ticket)
Financial Action Titles use finance-native language appropriate for CFO (ROI, payback, capital efficiency)
Recommendation slide includes CFO Delay Cost Table showing cumulative cost of not renewing
Language is calibrated for CFO (not general management defaults)
SCQA structure is maintained throughout
